MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT
THIS MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT (the "Agreement") is entered into as of [Date]
(the "Effective Date").
BETWEEN:
1. TIP Solutions Inc., a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the
State of Delaware, with its principal office at [Your Company Address] ("Provider");
AND
2. Zenith UK Solutions Ltd, a company registered in England and Wales
(Company Number: [Fake Co. No., e.g., 01234567]) with its registered office at
[Customer's Fake UK Address, e.g., 25 City Road, London, EC1Y 2AB, United Kingdom]
("Customer").
(Provider and Customer are each referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the
"Parties").
RECITALS
WHEREAS, Provider provides access to a proprietary software-as-a-service platform
known as "TIP" and related services;
WHEREAS, Customer desires to procure such services for its business operations; and
WHEREAS, the Parties wish to establish the terms and conditions under which Provider
will act as a Data Processor for Personal Data provided by the Customer, who acts as
the Data Controller.
NOW, THEREFORE, the Parties agree as follows:
1. DEFINITIONS
1.1 "Agreement" means this Master Services Agreement, including all Schedules and
Order Forms.
1.2 "Confidential Information" means all non-public information disclosed by a Party,
including Customer Data and the terms of this Agreement.
1.3 "Customer Data" means all electronic data, including Personal Data, submitted by
or for Customer to the Services.
1.4 "Data Protection Legislation" means all applicable data protection and privacy
laws, including (i) the UK GDPR (meaning the General Data Protection Regulation (EU)
2016/679 as it forms part of the law of England and Wales, Scotland, and Northern
Ireland by virtue of section 3 of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018); (ii) the
Data Protection Act 2018; and (iii) all other applicable laws and regulations relating to
the Processing of Personal Data and privacy, including the Privacy and Electronic
Communications Regulations (PECR).
1.5 "Data Subject", "Data Controller", "Data Processor", "Personal Data",
"Processing", and "Personal Data Breach" shall have the meanings ascribed to them
in the Data Protection Legislation.
1.6 "ICO" means the UK's Information Commissioner's Office.
1.7 "Order Form" means a document executed by both Parties specifying the Services,
fees, subscription term, and other commercial terms.
1.8 "Restricted Transfer" means a transfer of Personal Data from the UK to a third
country (such as the United States) which is not subject to an adequacy regulation.
1.9 "Services" means the "TIP" software-as-a-service platform.
1.10 "UK SCCs" means the applicable standard contractual clauses for international
transfers of Personal Data originating from the UK, as issued by the ICO or as an
addendum to EU SCCs.
1.11 "UK-US Data Bridge" means the UK extension to the EU-US Data Privacy
Framework, providing a lawful basis for transferring Personal Data to certified US
organisations.
2. SERVICES AND ORDER FORMS
2.1 Services. Provider shall provide the Services to Customer as set forth in the
applicable Order Form, which shall be governed by this Agreement.
2.2 Customer Responsibilities. Customer warrants that (a) it has all necessary rights
to provide the Customer Data to the Provider; and (b) its use of the Services and
instructions to the Provider shall comply with all applicable laws, including the Data
Protection Legislation.
3. FEES AND PAYMENT
3.1 Fees. Customer shall pay all fees specified in the Order Form. All Fees are quoted
and payable in Pounds Sterling (GBP).
3.2 Taxes. Fees are exclusive of Value Added Tax (VAT) and all other taxes, which
Customer shall pay at the applicable rate.
4. DATA PROTECTION (PROCESSOR OBLIGATIONS)
4.1 Roles of the Parties. The Parties acknowledge that for the purposes of the Data
Protection Legislation, Customer is the Data Controller and Provider is the Data
Processor of any Personal Data Processed in connection with the Services.
4.2 Provider's Processing Obligations. Provider shall, in relation to any Personal Data
Processed on behalf of the Customer:
(a) Process only on Instructions: Process the Personal Data only on the documented
written instructions of the Customer (as set forth in this Agreement and any Order
Form), unless required to do so by applicable law. Provider shall immediately inform
Customer if, in its opinion, an instruction infringes the Data Protection Legislation.
(b) Confidentiality: Ensure that all personnel authorised to Process Personal Data are
subject to a binding duty of confidentiality.
(c) Security: Implement and maintain the appropriate technical and organisational
measures (TOMs) specified in Section 5 to protect the Personal Data against a
Personal Data Breach.
(d) Sub-processors: Not engage any third-party sub-processor without the prior specific
written consent of the Customer. Provider shall maintain an up-to-date list of its sub-
processors, which shall be provided to Customer upon request. Where consent is
granted, Provider shall enter into a written agreement with the sub-processor imposing
data protection obligations no less protective than those in this Agreement. Provider
shall remain fully liable to Customer for any act or omission of its sub-processors.
(e) Data Subject Rights: Provide prompt and reasonable assistance to Customer (at
Customer's expense) to enable Customer to respond to requests from Data Subjects
exercising their rights under the Data Protection Legislation (e.g., right of access,
rectification, erasure).
(f) Assistance to Controller: Provide reasonable assistance to Customer in ensuring
compliance with its obligations under Articles 32 to 36 of the UK GDPR (relating to
security, breach notifications, and Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs)), taking
into account the nature of the Processing.
(g) Termination: Upon termination of this Agreement, at the written direction of the
Customer, Provider shall securely delete or return all Personal Data to the Customer
and delete existing copies, unless applicable law requires storage of the Personal Data.
5. SECURITY AND AUDIT
5.1 Technical and Organisational Measures (TOMs). Provider shall implement and
maintain appropriate TOMs to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk,
including but not limited to:
(a) The encryption of Personal Data in transit and at rest.
(b) The ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience
of processing systems.
(c) Measures to conduct regular testing and evaluation of the effectiveness of its
security measures (e.g., vulnerability scanning, penetration testing).
(d) Strict access controls, including multi-factor authentication, to limit access to
Personal Data to authorised personnel only.
5.2 Audit Rights. Provider shall make available to Customer, upon reasonable request
(no more than once annually), all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with
its obligations under this Section 5 and Section 4 (Data Protection). This includes
providing summaries of its most recent third-party audit reports (e.g., SOC 2 Type II,
ISO 27001). Customer (or a mutually agreed third-party auditor) may conduct a more
detailed audit at Customer's expense, with 30 days' notice, and subject to reasonable
confidentiality procedures.
6. INTERNATIONAL DATA TRANSFERS
6.1 Processing Location. Customer acknowledges that the Services are hosted in the
United States and that Processing of Personal Data will involve a Restricted Transfer
from the UK to the USA.
6.2 Lawful Transfer Mechanism. To ensure such a Restricted Transfer is lawful,
Provider warrants that:
(a) It is, and shall remain, certified under the UK-US Data Bridge (the UK extension to
the EU-US Data Privacy Framework) for the duration of this Agreement.
(b) Provider shall promptly notify Customer in writing if it fails to maintain, or is no longer
entitled to rely on, its UK-US Data Bridge certification.
(c) Fallback Mechanism: In the event the UK-US Data Bridge is invalidated, suspended,
or Provider's certification lapses, the Parties agree to cooperate in good faith to
immediately execute the prevailing UK SCCs (including the UK Addendum to the EU
SCCs, if applicable) to ensure the continued lawfulness of the transfer.
7. PERSONAL DATA BREACH
7.1 Notification to Customer. Provider shall notify Customer in writing without undue
delay, and in no event later than thirty-six (36) hours, after becoming aware of any
confirmed Personal Data Breach affecting Customer Data.
7.2 Cooperation. Such notification shall include all available information to assist
Customer in meeting its own breach notification obligations to the ICO and affected
Data Subjects. Provider shall provide full and prompt cooperation with Customer's
investigation and remediation of the breach.
7.3 Control. Customer shall have sole control over the breach response, including any
decision to notify the ICO or Data Subjects. Provider shall not make any public
statement about a Personal Data Breach without Customer's prior written consent.
8. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
8.1 Exclusion. In no event shall either Party be liable for any indirect, special, or
consequential damages (including loss of profits).
8.2 Liability Cap. Subject to Section 8.3, each Party's total aggregate liability arising
from this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid or payable by Customer in the
twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.
8.3 Exceptions (Super-Cap). The limitations in Section 8.2 shall not apply to: (a) a
Party's gross negligence or willful misconduct; or (b) a Party's breach of its
confidentiality obligations.
* Provided that, for any breach of a Party's obligations under Section 4 (Data
Protection), Section 6 (International Data Transfers), or Section 7 (Personal Data
Breach), that Party's total aggregate liability shall be capped at a "super-cap" of three
times (3x) the total fees paid or payable in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.
9. TERM AND TERMINATION
9.1 Term. This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until all
Order Forms have expired or been terminated.
9.2 Termination for Cause. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for a material
breach that is not cured within 30 days of notice. A significant breach of Section 4 or 7,
or a failure to maintain a lawful transfer mechanism under Section 6, shall be deemed a
material breach.
10. GENERAL PROVISIONS
10.1 Governing Law. This Agreement, and any dispute or claim (including non-
contractual disputes) arising out of or in connection with it, shall be governed by and
construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales.
10.2 Jurisdiction. Each Party irrevocably agrees that the courts of England and Wales
shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim (including non-
contractual disputes) arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
10.3 Modern Slavery. Each Party warrants that it complies with the UK Modern Slavery
Act 2015 and shall not engage in any practice that would constitute an offence under
that Act.
10.4 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the
Parties and supersedes all prior agreements.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement by their duly
authorized representatives.

CUSTOMER:
TIP Solutions Inc.
Zenith UK Solutions Ltd
